This article was created as part of my submission to the #H0Hackathon.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Maintaining Open Source Is More Than Writing Code
When people think about open source, they usually think about writing code.
Maintainers know that’s only half the job.
The real work starts after the code is merged:

• Reviewing pull requests

• Triaging issues

• Updating documentation

• Preparing releases

• Prioritizing what should be worked on next

• Helping contributors

• Keeping repositories healthy
Most of these tasks are repetitive, yet they consume a huge portion of a maintainer’s time.
I wanted to answer a simple question:
What if AI could become a maintainer’s operating system instead of just another chatbot?
That idea became MaintainerOS.

The Problem
Current AI tools are great at generating code.
They’re not great at understanding repository workflows.
A maintainer doesn’t just ask:
“Write me a README.”
They ask questions like:

• Which pull request should I merge first?

• Which issue should I fix today?

• Is this repository ready for a release?

• Which documentation is outdated?

• Can this change be automated safely?
MaintainerOS focuses on those problems.

Building MaintainerOS
The application is built using:

• Next.js

• React

• TypeScript

• Tailwind CSS

• shadcn/ui

• Vercel AI SDK

• GitHub OAuth

• GitHub REST API

• AWS Aurora PostgreSQL

• Prisma ORM
The frontend is deployed on Vercel while Aurora PostgreSQL stores repository metadata, execution history, workspace state, and user settings.

AI Is Only One Part of the System
One of the biggest lessons from this project was realizing that AI shouldn’t directly modify repositories.
Instead, I built an Action Engine.
Every workflow follows the same lifecycle:
Planner
↓
Validator
↓
Executor
↓
ReporterThe AI proposes a plan.
The validator checks whether it’s safe.
The executor performs the GitHub operations.
The reporter summarizes what happened.
This makes the system extensible while keeping maintainers in control.

Repository Automation
One of my favorite workflows is documentation automation.
MaintainerOS can:

• Analyze a repository

• Generate an AI execution plan

• Show a reviewable diff

• Create a branch

• Commit the changes

• Open a Pull Request

• Detect when it has been merged

• Refresh repository analysis

• Recommend the next maintenance task
Instead of simply generating text, the AI completes an entire maintenance workflow.

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Architechture DiagramWhy I Switched to the Vercel AI SDK
Initially, the project was tightly coupled to Gemini.
That quickly became a limitation.
During development I repeatedly hit free-tier API limits.
Migrating to the Vercel AI SDK allowed me to redesign the AI layer around providers instead of models.
Today MaintainerOS supports:

• Google Gemini

• Groq

• OpenRouter
It also supports Bring Your Own Key (BYOK), allowing maintainers to use their own AI provider instead of relying on a shared server quota.
The AI layer is now model-agnostic and future-proof.

Why AWS Aurora PostgreSQL?
Although GitHub remains the source of truth for repository data, MaintainerOS still needs durable application storage.
Aurora PostgreSQL stores:

• User profiles

• Repository metadata

• Workspace preferences

• AI execution history

• Cached analyses

• Action state
This allows the application to maintain continuity across workflows while benefiting from a managed relational database.

Biggest Challenges
The hardest part wasn’t AI.
It was building trust.
Maintainers shouldn’t wonder:
“Did AI just modify my repository?”
Instead they should always know:

• what changed

• why it changed

• how it changed
Every automated workflow is reviewable before execution.
That philosophy influenced almost every technical decision in the project.

What I Learned
This hackathon fundamentally changed how I think about AI products.
The value isn’t in generating text.
The value is in orchestrating reliable workflows around AI.
I also gained hands-on experience with:

• GitHub automation

• Streaming AI interfaces

• AWS Aurora PostgreSQL

• AI provider abstraction

• GitHub OAuth

• Safe execution pipelines

• Full-stack application architecture

What’s Next
MaintainerOS is just getting started.
The roadmap includes:

• AI-generated issue fixes

• Security remediation workflows

• Release preparation

• GitLab support

• Team workspaces

• GitHub webhooks

• Repository memory

• Enterprise AI governance
The long-term vision is simple:
Build the AI workspace that every open source maintainer opens before GitHub.
If you’d like to follow the project or share feedback, I’d love to hear your thoughts.
Happy building! 🚀
